response.justification: While this candidate demonstrates strong software engineering fundamentals and craftsmanship across diverse technologies, they are fundamentally misaligned with this role's core requirements. The position explicitly requires senior C++ expertise as its foundation; this candidate has only six months of C++ experience (2014–2015), with their recent 11+ years focused on JavaScript/TypeScript, DLang, and web/backend systems. Additionally, the lack of manufacturing, industrial automation, or IoT domain experience—combined with the absence of Secure Development Lifecycle or IEC 62443 expertise—creates a substantial ramp-up burden inappropriate for a senior-level role.
strengths: Demonstrates strong software craftsmanship with proven experience in TDD, CI/CD, and code quality practices across 17+ years of diverse roles.
Extensive architectural and systems thinking shown through microservices work, event-driven platforms, and mentoring others through technical workshops and code dojos.
Versatile full-stack engineer capable of learning new technologies rapidly, with exposure to backend systems, DevOps, and infrastructure tooling.
concerns: Critical gap in primary technical requirement: only six months of C++ experience from 2014–2015, with recent 11+ years focused entirely on JavaScript/TypeScript and higher-level languages.

Zero demonstrated experience in manufacturing, industrial automation, IoT applications, or related domains; no familiarity with industrial protocols (Modbus, IEC 61850, OPC UA) or cybersecurity standards (IEC 62443).
No evidence of Secure Development Lifecycle practices, secure coding expertise, or industrial cybersecurity knowledge required for the role's compliance and security responsibilities.
